text: All developing and developed countries have to conduct their economies with the rules of sustainable development in order to achieve the desired economic development. In addition to the environmental damage caused in the course of the development process in the wake of the industrial revolution, which marked a turning point in the history of humanity and in which humanity has undergone a tremendous transformation, has also caused inevitable damage to nature due to the unconscious use of primary products and the use of fossil products. However, not all members of the society could benefit from the improvements brought with this development process and the employment created was generally not sufficient to absorb the increasing labor force. This study aims to examine the impact of labor market on the green jobs created in recent years since 1980s and thus on the realization of sustainable development and the absorption of the increasing labor force.
explanation: The text shows plausible academic tone with minor non-native phrasing (e.g., 'since 1980s'), a pattern more common in human writing; AI-generated text would more likely be smoother or overly polished. This ambiguity slightly favors human authorship.
